title: Source lineage for the Life of Saint Brendan in Martha Bälz's 1909 edition
summary: CME00141 is the University of Michigan encoding of the title page and printed pp. 1-70 of Martha Bälz's 1909 critical edition of the Middle English Life of Saint Brendan. Bälz used Ashmole 43 (M) as her base text, normally retaining it and emending by stated criteria, and compared eleven further witnesses through a mixture of earlier printed editions and copies made by Arnold Hahn. DIMEV's five additional witnesses enlarge the modern census but are not retroactive sources of Bälz's edition. Complete public manuscript surrogates and every identified public selection object were cached where available; unpublicized surfaces and provider-controlled reproductions remain explicitly requestable or unresolved.
review_notes: Work, manuscript, edition, facsimile, and encoding layers are distinct; witness dates and dialects are not transferred to the abstract work.
Siglum expansions follow Bälz's own key on preliminary pp. i-ii. Apparatus initials alone were not used to infer unidentified sources.
Coverage describes the full accessed object. A complete selection bundle remains partial coverage of its manuscript witness.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Twelve manuscript witnesses in Bälz's census
editorial_practices.representation: M (Ashmole 43) as the running base text with eleven comparison witnesses in footnotes
editorial_practices.description: Bälz presents a base-text critical edition, not a diplomatic reproduction of one codex and not a witness-neutral eclectic text.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Readings of M and the grouped comparison witnesses
editorial_practices.representation: M retained except where source/context or cross-group agreement under stated linguistic criteria supports change
editorial_practices.description: Bälz explicitly identifies changes to M at lines 8, 168, 177, 257, 290, 478, and 655 and says I normally supplies an adequate replacement. Apparatus agreement alone must not be mistaken for a wholly eclectic method.

editorial_practices.source_feature: H, W, M, C, A, L, R, G, O, T, I, and B
editorial_practices.representation: Sigla keyed on preliminary pp. i-ii and variant readings in 771 encoded notes
editorial_practices.description: H is mediated by Horstmann 1887, W by Wright 1844, M and supported B readings by Horstmann 1874, and C/A/L/R/G/O/T/I by Arnold Hahn's copies. B is explicitly excepted from Hahn's copying statement, so direct Bälz consultation remains unresolved.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Latin and French Brendan narrative parallels
editorial_practices.representation: Bracket locators and introductory comparison keyed to Jubinal 1836
editorial_practices.description: The apparatus distinguishes textual witness variants from comparative source locators. Bälz argues for a Latin Navigatio family-g source and a French verse source close to Jubinal's text, and tentatively proposes knowledge of an Anglo-Norman poem; these are work-level hypotheses, not identified physical source objects.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Bälz title page and printed pp. 1-70 with running text and dense footnote apparatus
editorial_practices.representation: Two DIV1 elements, one LG, 738 L elements, 771 NOTE elements, 71 PB elements, and typographic HI markup
editorial_practices.description: The encoding preserves the selected title page, poem, page sequence, and apparatus but excludes the xxxii-page introduction. XML language and structural counts describe this representation, not the whole publication or abstract work.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00141.xml, at CME submodule commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the CME identifiers, Bälz title page, promotion date, encoded poem and apparatus, printed-page mapping, and file boundary.

evidence.citation: Programmatic structural audit of the pinned CME00141 XML.
evidence.locator: DIV1, DIV2, HEAD, LG, L, NOTE, PB, MILESTONE, P, HI, ITEM, and ITEM1 counts
evidence.summary: The XML contains two DIV1 elements, no DIV2 or HEAD, one LG, 738 L, 771 NOTE, 71 PB, no MILESTONE, five P, 7,459 HI, one ITEM, and no ITEM1; the 738 lines form one complete poem and the notes are apparatus rather than additional works.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection description and title list.
evidence.summary: Describes the public corpus as keyed from public-domain editions and encoded in basic TEI, binds CME00141 to Bälz 1909, and applies a No Copyright - United States statement to collection materials.

evidence.citation: Martha Bälz, Die me. Brendanlegende des Gloucesterlegendars (Berlin, 1909), title page and printed pp. 1-70.
evidence.locator: PDF pp. 1-71; title page and printed pp. 1-70
evidence.summary: Primary edition evidence for the exact text and apparatus encoded in CME00141, the 738-line numbering, the thesis title page, and the fact that the legacy PDF omits the xxxii-page introduction.

evidence.summary: Supplies Bälz's responsibility, Mayer & Müller imprint, Berlin 1909 publication, doctoral-thesis note, and complete physical extent xxxii, 70 pages.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of Bälz 1909 preliminary pp. i-ii in the free Google Books viewer.
evidence.summary: Expands Bälz's sigla H, W, M, C, A, L, R, G, O, T, I, and B; identifies Horstmann 1887 for H, Wright 1844 for W, Horstmann 1874 for M, and states that Arnold Hahn copied C/A/L/R/G/O/T/I while explicitly excepting B.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of Bälz 1909 preliminary p. v in the free Google Books viewer.
evidence.summary: States that M is the oldest good manuscript and base text; lists changes at lines 8, 168, 177, 257, 290, 478, and 655; gives agreement-and-linguistic criteria for other changes; and says I supplies an adequate replacement when M must be abandoned.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of Bälz 1909 preliminary pp. iii-v in the free Google Books viewer.
evidence.summary: Keys bracket locators to Jubinal 1836; argues for a Latin Navigatio family-g text and a French verse source close to Jubinal's text; tentatively proposes knowledge of an Anglo-Norman poem; and treats French prose as comparison rather than an asserted source.

evidence.citation: Carl Horstmann, 'Die altenglische Legende von St. Brendan aus Ms. Ashmol. 43, fol. 71b,' Archiv 53 (1874): 17-48.
evidence.summary: Supports the Ashmole base transcription and Harley/Wright, Laud, and Bodley comparison material that Bälz inherited, including printed B readings as a supported intermediary.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for archivfrdasstu53brauuoft.
evidence.summary: Identifies the complete scanned volume and derivative files and records not-in-copyright status; inspected title and contents establish the relevant volume as 1874 despite generic metadata noise.

evidence.citation: Thomas Wright, ed., St. Brandan: A Medieval Legend of the Sea, in English Verse and Prose (London, 1844).
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for Wright's transcription of the Harley MS 2277 metrical Life, the printed intermediary explicitly cited by Bälz for W.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for stbrandanamedie01musegoog.
evidence.summary: Identifies the complete publication scan, OCR, and metadata derivatives and supports the public-domain assessment for the book.

evidence.citation: Carl Horstmann, ed., The Early South-English Legendary, EETS OS 87 (London, 1887).
evidence.locator: Laud Misc. 108 Brendan text, printed pp. 220-240
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the H text and the printed intermediary Bälz identifies for Laud Misc. 108.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for earlysouthenglis00hors.
evidence.summary: Identifies the complete EETS volume and PDF derivative used for the local whole-object cache.

evidence.citation: Achille Jubinal, La Légende Latine de S. Brandaines (Paris, 1836).
evidence.locator: complete volume; French prose from p. 57 and French verse from p. 105
evidence.summary: Supplies the Latin and French texts to which Bälz's bracket locators and source discussion refer; it is comparative edition evidence, not the medieval author's identified physical exemplar.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for LaLegendeSBrandaines.
evidence.summary: Identifies the complete public-domain publication scan and its exact PDF derivative.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, record 4567 and version 1.0 data release.
evidence.summary: Supplies the normalized incipit, 738-line aa-couplet description, bibliography, exact loci for seventeen witnesses, and a census five witnesses larger than Bälz's twelve-source edition.

evidence.citation: PCMEP, text details for Saint Brendan.
evidence.summary: Provides a 1250-1290 composition range with circa 1260 suggested, broad southern dialect assessment, septenary aa-couplet form, identifiers, and a 733-line convention described as 732 lines plus final Amen.

evidence.citation: Simon Lavery, 'The Source of the St Brendan Story in the South English Legendary,' Leeds Studies in English, n.s. 15 (1984): 21-32.
evidence.summary: Argues that the Latin Navigatio is probably the principal source, rejects direct use of Benedeit's Anglo-Norman Voyage as a secondary source, characterizes the concise didactic adaptation, and reports a 734-line extent.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, MS. Add. C. 38.
evidence.locator: Life of St Brendan fragment, f. 109v
evidence.summary: Supplies the current shelfmark, 1400-1410 date, fragmentary extent, work locus, and absence of a linked public surrogate.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, MS. Ashmole 43.
evidence.summary: Supplies the current shelfmark, 1300-1325 date, work boundary, and manuscript description for Bälz's M base witness.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, MS. Bodley 779.
evidence.locator: Life of St Brendan, ff. 1r-1v and 3r-10r
evidence.summary: Supplies the current 1425-1450 date, correcting Bälz's sixteenth-century estimate, and the split work boundary for B.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, MS. Laud Misc. 108.
evidence.locator: surviving Life of St Brendan, ff. 104r-110r
evidence.summary: Supplies the broad 1290-1400 codex history, target boundary, early-core qualification, and physical lacuna for H.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, MS. Laud Misc. 463.
evidence.summary: Supplies the 1390-1400 date and target boundary for L; its one public selected image is outside the work.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, MS. Tanner 17.
evidence.summary: Supplies the 1400-1460 date and target boundary for T; the selected digital object covers only duplicated boundary exposures.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian complete IIIF manifests for MS Ashmole 43, MS Bodley 779, and MS Laud Misc. 108.
evidence.summary: The exact provider manifests and cached inventories establish complete 580-, 672-, and 496-canvas codex sequences and map Brendan to Ashmole canvases 156-174, Bodley canvases 9-10 and 13-27, and Laud canvases 219-231.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian selected-object IIIF manifests for Ashmole 43, Laud Misc. 108, Laud Misc. 463, and Tanner 17.
evidence.summary: Every image in each separate public selection object was cached. Ashmole/Laud 108/Laud 463 selections have zero Brendan overlap; Tanner's 46-image object includes only duplicate exposures of work boundary folios 99v and 107v.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian, Terms of Use.
evidence.summary: Explains that most item images are CC BY-NC 4.0 with attribution and noncommercial conditions and that commercial uses or high-resolution requests require Imaging Services; exact manifests were checked for item-level application.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Special collections mediated copying.
evidence.summary: Provides a request route for reference scans of manuscripts, with mediated.copying@bodleian.ox.ac.uk, per-scan fees for external users, and Imaging Services terms.

evidence.citation: Richard Gameson, The Medieval Manuscripts of Trinity College, Oxford (2018), MS 57 catalog PDF.
evidence.locator: MS 57; South English Legendary and Brendan ff. 39r-46r
evidence.summary: The current official catalog describes a southern English manuscript from the fourth quarter of the fourteenth century after 1375; the automated request returned a verification challenge and was not bypassed.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Oxford, Special collections.
evidence.summary: States that pre-Reformation manuscripts are described in the Oxford catalog, held at the Bodleian Weston Library, and available through appointment arrangements and College Librarian enquiries.

evidence.citation: Parker Library on the Web, Corpus Christi College Cambridge MS 145.
evidence.summary: Identifies the codex, its fourteenth-century South English Legendary content, and the complete digital object; Bälz's C text occupies ff. 67v-77r.

evidence.citation: Parker Library on the Web IIIF manifest for CCCC MS 145.
evidence.summary: Supplies the complete codex sequence, exact work mapping, provider attribution, and CC BY-NC 4.0 terms; visual inspection confirms the Brendan/Dunstan boundary on f. 77r.

evidence.citation: Manuscripts of the West Midlands, Pepys Library MS 2344.
evidence.summary: Describes Pepys MS 2344 as a second-half-fourteenth-century South English Legendary witness with a Gloucester scribal dialect. DIMEV supplies the Brendan locus pp. 273-288.

evidence.citation: Magdalene College, Pepys Digital Library.
evidence.summary: Lists the manuscripts currently digitized, which do not include MS 2344; supplies the Pepys Librarian contact and states that images must be purchased and permission obtained for publication.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge, James Catalogue of Western Manuscripts, R.3.25.
evidence.summary: Supplies the fifteenth-century date, shelfmark, South English Legendary contents, exact Brendan boundary, complete IIIF route, and CC BY-NC 4.0 license.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge IIIF manifest for R.3.25.
evidence.summary: Supplies a complete 586-canvas codex sequence with exact source inventory and maps Brendan to canvases 461-476.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Add MS 10301.
evidence.summary: Supplies the current shelfmark, broad fourteenth-century date, physical item request route, and absence of public digital content; DIMEV supplies ff. 80r-90v.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Cotton MS Julius D IX.
evidence.summary: Supplies the first-half-fifteenth-century date, South English Legendary extent, current request route, and explicit notice that digital images are unavailable; DIMEV supplies ff. 74v-83v.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Egerton MS 2891.
evidence.summary: Supplies the 1300-1340 date, Brendan boundary ff. 77v-86, missing leaf after f. 83, and absence of public images.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Harley MS 2277.
evidence.summary: Supplies the circa-1300 date (1295-1305), South-West English origin, physical request route, and an unavailable legacy digitized-content link; DIMEV supplies ff. 41v-51r.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, access and request guidance.
evidence.summary: States that manuscript requests require a Reader Pass registered or renewed since 21 March 2024, that manuscripts are consulted in London, and that some legacy digitized content is currently unavailable during service recovery.

evidence.citation: British Library Digitisation Services, imaging terms, online order form, and 2025-2026 pricing.
evidence.summary: Current evidence for requestable color manuscript photography, the exact online order route, the fee schedule, exact-folio requirements, and separate publication licensing.

evidence.citation: Lambeth Palace Library archives catalog, MSS/223, Legendary in English Verse.
evidence.summary: Supplies the current MS 223 shelfmark, title, vellum extent, and broad fourteenth-century institutional date. DIMEV supplies the Brendan boundary ff. 112v-122v; Bälz's early-fifteenth-century estimate is retained as a historical conflict.

evidence.citation: Lambeth Palace Library LUNA MS223* selection inventory and three compound IIIF objects.
evidence.locator: 87 single records plus 10-, 20-, and 15-canvas compound selections; Brendan only at compound selection canvas 15, f. 112v
evidence.summary: Every current single-image record and every canvas of all three published compound selections was cached. Only f. 112v overlaps Brendan; no public images of ff. 113r-122v were found.

evidence.citation: Lambeth Palace Library, Imaging Services and LUNA image records.
evidence.summary: Provides the mediated imaging route. LUNA identifies Lambeth Palace Library but supplies no open license; permission and fees must be checked for reproduction or publication.

evidence.citation: Sian Prosser, Ripon Cathedral Manuscript Fragments handlist, entry 33.
evidence.summary: Identifies a parchment strip from the South English Legendary, dates it to the early fifteenth century, assigns a northern dialect, and describes the end of Brendan followed by the beginning of Dunstan; the work is printed in EETS 87, pp. 204-205.

evidence.citation: Margaret Mullett, The Armor of God: The South English Legendary and Its Sermon Tradition (Cornell University dissertation, 2017).
evidence.summary: Supplies scholarly dates 1350-1400 for Wisbech MS 21 and circa 1500 for Ripon; the latter conflicts with the current Prosser handlist's early-fifteenth-century date.

evidence.citation: University of Leeds Library, published photograph of Ripon Cathedral Fragment 33,1.
evidence.summary: Shows the final nine Brendan line beginnings followed by Dunstan. The JPEG is the complete public selection object, but the reverse and a complete two-sided surrogate are not public.

evidence.citation: University of Leeds Library Special Collections, Ripon Cathedral Manuscript Fragments.
evidence.summary: Identifies the collection, makes access unrestricted through visitor requests, exposes the Prosser handlist, and states that supply does not confer reproduction rights and written consent is required for publication or reuse.

evidence.citation: Wisbech & Fenland Museum, contact and Image Use and Licensing pages.
evidence.summary: Supplies the curator contact, states that no public item-level record or facsimile was found, requires permission to reproduce collection images, and offers licensed new photography for a fee.

open_questions.question: Can a stable, complete copy of Bälz's 1909 dissertation, including all thirty-two preliminary pages, be acquired through a provider route that can be recorded without defeating an automated verification challenge?
next_steps: Request a complete scan through a holding library or an authenticated Google Books/HathiTrust workflow.
Retain the cached 71-page title-and-edition selection as a complete local copy only of its stated selection boundary, not of the whole dissertation.
Do not bypass CAPTCHA or provider verification controls.

open_questions.question: Do Arnold Hahn's manuscript copies survive, what were their precise production and transmission histories, and did Bälz consult Bodley 779 directly rather than through Horstmann's 1874 edition?
next_steps: Search Bälz and Hahn archival papers and correspondence for the copies and collation notes.
Compare Bälz's B apparatus against Horstmann 1874 and Bodley 779.
Keep Horstmann 1874 as the supported B intermediary and Hahn's copies as the supported C/A/L/R/G/O/T/I intermediaries until direct consultation is evidenced.

open_questions.question: Which Latin Navigatio branch or exemplar underlies the poem, and how should Bälz's tentative Anglo-Norman source proposal be reconciled with Lavery's rejection of direct dependence on Benedeit's Voyage?
next_steps: Compare distinctive readings against the Latin Navigatio recensions and the French verse tradition represented by Jubinal.
Consult scholarship after Lavery 1984 for a revised source model.
Keep Bälz's and Lavery's arguments as conflicting scholarly hypotheses rather than converting either into an identified physical source.

open_questions.question: Can complete public or permission-cleared image coverage be obtained for the Brendan loci not covered by the currently available digital selections?
next_steps: Request the relevant leaves of Add. C. 38, Laud Misc. 463, Tanner 17, Trinity Oxford MS 57, Pepys MS 2344, and the four British Library witnesses through their repositories.
Request Lambeth MS 223 ff. 113r-122v, the reverse of Ripon Fragment 33, and Wisbech MS 21 through their mediated imaging routes.
Record supplied-image licenses and terms before redistribution.

open_questions.question: How should Bälz's and DIMEV's 738-line extent, Lavery's 734-line extent, PCMEP's 732 lines plus final Amen, and the 733-line CME00107 XML count be reconciled?
next_steps: Collate lineation in Bälz, the source editions, and representative manuscripts.
Record whether rubrics, Amen, split lines, omissions, and witness-specific additions are counted.
Keep the 738 numbered lines in CME00141 separate from abstract-work, witness, and alternate-edition counts.
